section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_concurrent_calls module provides per-account concurrent call limiting for &osips;. It tracks active calls per account (typically identified by From-&uri; username) and enforces configurable per-account limits loaded from a CSV file.
Accounts not listed in the limits file are subject to a configurable default limit. An account with a limit of 0 is effectively blocked from making any calls.

section.title: How it works
section.para: The module uses explicit increment/decrement calls from the &osips; script rather than dialog callbacks. This design keeps the module simple and gives the script author full control over when counts are adjusted.

Typical usage:
On initial INVITE (no to-tag): call check_concurrent() to verify the account is under its limit. If allowed, call concurrent_inc() to increment the counter.
On failure responses (3xx-6xx to INVITE): call concurrent_dec() to release the slot.
On BYE: call concurrent_dec() to release the slot.

Per-account limits are loaded from a CSV file at worker startup. Each worker maintains its own independent set of counters, so the effective limit is per-worker. For precise global limiting, consider using an external data store.
The check_concurrent() function sets $var(concurrent_count) and $var(concurrent_limit) pseudo-variables for use in script logging or response messages.
Limits can be reloaded at runtime using concurrent_reload() without restarting &osips;. Active call counters are preserved across reloads; only the per-account limits are updated.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
None.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None.

section.title: limits_file (string)
section.para: Path to a CSV file defining per-account concurrent call limits. Format: one line per account as account,max_calls. Lines starting with # are treated as comments. Blank lines are ignored. This parameter is required. The module will fail to load if this parameter is not set or the file does not exist.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (must be set).

section.title: default_limit (integer)
section.para: Default concurrent call limit applied to accounts not listed in the limits file. Must be at least 0; a value of 0 blocks all unlisted accounts.

Check whether the given account is under its concurrent call limit. On return, the pseudo-variables $var(concurrent_count) and $var(concurrent_limit) are set to the current count and the effective limit for the account.
This function does NOT increment the counter. Use concurrent_inc() separately after a successful check.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier to check. Typically $fU (From-&uri; username) or $rU (Request-&uri; username). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -1 (denied) - account is at or over its limit (blocked).
-2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: check_concurrent usage

section.para: Increment the concurrent call counter for the given account. Call this after a successful check_concurrent() when the INVITE is being forwarded.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. Must match the value used in check_concurrent(). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.
Decrement the concurrent call counter for the given account. The counter floors at zero and will never go negative.
Call this when a call ends (BYE) or when an INVITE receives a failure response and the counter was previously incremented.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: account (string) - The account identifier. Must match the value used in concurrent_inc(). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

Re-read the limits CSV file from disk and atomically swap the per-account limits. Active call counters are preserved; only the limit values are updated. This allows changing account limits without restarting &osips;.

The reload affects only the worker process that executes the function. To reload all workers, trigger this function from a route that is processed by each worker (e.g., via an MI-triggered OPTIONS ping or timer route).
On success, the accounts and reloads statistics are updated.
Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - file read error or worker not initialized.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: concurrent_reload usage

section.para: Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(concurrent_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

section.title: $var(concurrent_count)
section.para: The current number of active calls for the checked account. Set as an integer by check_concurrent().

section.title: $var(concurrent_limit)
section.para: The effective concurrent call limit for the checked account. Set as an integer by check_concurrent(). This is either the per-account limit from the limits file or the default_limit if the account is not listed.

section.title: $var(concurrent_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by concurrent_stats().
Total number of concurrent call checks performed.
Number of checks where the account was under its limit.
Number of checks where the account was at or over its limit.
Total number of counter increments (concurrent_inc() calls).
Total number of counter decrements (concurrent_dec() calls).
Number of accounts loaded from the limits file. Updated on reload.
Number of successful hot reloads performed via concurrent_reload().
